request.question: What are our top 3 positions in the public equities book? Show the sell-side analyst ratings on each, and how much I can add or trim per name before we hit our strategy bands on sector and geography.

events.text: ## Top 3 Positions, Public Equities Book (by market value, 30 June 2026

)

| Rank | Name | Sector | Market value | % of book |
|------|------|--------|-------------|-----------|
| 1 | Al Rajhi Bank | Financials

| SAR 588.0M | 31.9% |
| 2 | Saudi Aramco | Energy | SAR 278.9M | 15.1% |
| 3 | SABIC | Materials
Al Rajhi Bank holds 6,000,000 shares
at a SAR 98.00 close for SAR 588.00M (31.9% of book); Saudi Aramco holds 11,200,000 shares at SAR
24.90 for SAR 278.88M (15.1%); SABIC holds 3,750,000 shares at SAR 66.40 for SAR 249.00M (13.5%).

events.text: Note on methodology:
consensus TP is the arithmetic mean of the
covering houses' 12-month targets, with upside measured vs the 30 June 2026 close.

events.quote:
House ratings as published on each broker research portal; consensus TP is the arithmetic mean of the covering houses' 12-month targets;
upside vs the 30 June 2026 close.

events.text: ## Add / Trim Headroom to Strategy Bands

Headroom is enforced at the sector-band level; each name below inherits its sector's status.

**Al Raj
hi Bank (Financials): TRIM at least SAR 35.1M.**
Financials sits at 31.9%
vs a 22-30% band, above band.

events.quote:
Sector Market value (SAR M) Weight Band Status Headroom (SAR M)
Financials 588.00 31.9% 22-30% Above band Trim at least 35.1
Materials 374.65 20.3% 15-22% In band Add up to 30.8
Energy 278.88 15.1%

events.text: This name is also the single binding constraint:
no issuer may exceed 30% of the public book, and Al Rajhi Bank stands at 31.9%, above both the cap and the Financials band
top; a trim of at least SAR 35.1M restores both (to 30.0%).

events.quote:
Al Rajhi Bank stands at 31.9%, above both the
cap and the Financials band top; a trim of at least SAR 35.1M restores both (to 30.0%).

events.text: **Saudi Aramco (Energy): ADD up to SAR 52.
Energy sits at 15.1% vs a 10-18% band, in band, with headroom to add up to SAR 52.8M.

events.text: Note: this SAR 30.8M is the Materials-sector head
room shared across SABIC, SIPCHEM and Yansab combined, not ex
clusive to SABIC. Any single-name add would also need to respect the 30%-of-book issuer c

ap, though SABIC at 13.5% is well clear of that.

## Geography

There is no geographic add/trim room
to work with:
the book is 100.0% Saudi Arabia, and
the IC Charter mandates Tadawul-listed instruments only for the public book, so there is no non-KSA capacity under the current mandate; MENA
ex-KSA exposure would require a Charter amendment.

events.quote:
·
Geography: the book is 100.0% Saudi Arabia. The IC Charter mandates Tadawul-listed instruments only for the
public book, so there is no non-KSA capacity under the current mandate; MENA ex-KSA expo

events.text: A methodology flag wor
th noting for how these numbers should be actioned:
trim and add amounts are comput
ed holding total book value constant (trims redeployed within the book, additions funded from the liquidity sleeve).

events.quote:
·
Methodology: trim and add amounts are computed holding total book value constant (trims redeployed within the
book, additions funded from the liquidity sleeve).
